prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine (11 atoms: N + 5C + 5H) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF, tier 'standard', with density fitting) in the gas phase (no implicit solvation), charge 0 / multiplicity 1 for the neutral, charge +1 / mult 2 for the cation, charge −1 / mult 2 for the anion. Partial charges were obtained via Mulliken population analysis (PySCF). Geometry: user-supplied pyridine.xyz, used as-is (no re-optimization). All three SCF calculations converged; charge-conservation integrity checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). No warnings reported. Integrity: trustworthy = true.

Headline result (most electrophilic site): N (atom 1), f⁺ = 0.48422572.
Most nucleophilic site: C4 (atom 4), f⁻ = 0.11826260.

Full per-atom fukui_plus  = [0.48422572, 0.32447225, 0.14032093, 0.14039587, 0.29962626, 0.29947707, -0.1415132, -0.1336841, -0.13365905, -0.1398163, -0.13984545]
Full per-atom fukui_minus = [0.064122671, 0.0061877146, 0.11823139, 0.1182626, 0.11206595, 0.11200985, 0.08284851, 0.098179487, 0.09818226, 0.094960093, 0.094949479]
Full per-atom dual_descriptor = [0.42010305, 0.31828453, 0.022089545, 0.022133277, 0.18756031, 0.18746722, -0.22436171, -0.23186359, -0.23184131, -0.23477639, -0.23479493]

Method: B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF DFT backend, Mulliken charges, gas phase, finite-difference Fukui indices.
